data.title: Coinbase estimates up to $400M in customer payouts after data breach
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) expects to pay $180M-$400M in reimbursements and remediation after a recent data breach exposed sensitive customer information and led to users being tricked into sending funds to the attackers, it said on Thursday.

On May 11, the crypto exchange "received an email communication from an unknown threat actor claiming to have obtained information about certain Coinbase customer accounts, as well as internal Coinbase documentation, including materials relating to customer-service and account-management systems," it disclosed in an SEC filing.

The attackers allegedly paid overseas support staff to gain internal access and gather customer data. Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) noted it has already flagged and fired those involved.

From there, the cyber criminals demanded $20M to keep the stolen information private, though Coinbase (COIN) rejected the extortion attempt and instead put up a $20M reward for information leading to their arrest and conviction, according to a blog post.

In addition to the reward fund, the company has tagged the attackers' addresses so that the authorities can trace the stolen funds and work to recover stolen assets.

The scammers did not access login credentials, private keys, any ability to move or access customer funds, access to Coinbase Prime accounts, and access to any Coinbase or Coinbase customer hot or cold wallets.

COIN shares gapped down 7.6% in late morning trading.

data.title: Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust press release (OTC:APPTF): Q1 AFFO per Unit of $0.247 (diluted) and paid regular cash distributions of $0.201 per Unit.
  • Rental revenue in Q1 2025 increased by 2.1% to $23.9 million, compared to $23.4 million in Q1 2024. 
  • Net income and other comprehensive income of $7.6 million in Q1 2025, compared to $20.9 million in Q1 2024. 
  • FFO in Q1 2025 increased 4.1% to $12.6 million, or $0.251 per Unit.
  • AFFO in Q1 2025 increased 6.0% to $12.4 million, or $0.247 per Unit.

data.title: Investor uncertainty grows as market rallies: Bear trap or Bull run?
data.description:
Investor sentiment appears increasingly divided as questions loom over the future direction of Wall Street. While markets initially stumbled following "Liberation Day," they have since staged a notable rebound. Still, a growing number of market participants are left wondering: is this simply a bear market rally, or the beginning of a sustained climb?

For those caught between the fear of missing out on potential gains and the desire for downside protection, Seeking Alpha has compiled a list of stocks that may offer a balance of both safety and growth. 

Outlined below is a curated list of companies that can cover both the idea of growth and safety in the form of dividends. The criteria below covers U.S. stocks with market capitalizations of $100B or greater along with divided yields of at least 3% and Seeking Alpha quant grades of a B- or higher as it pertains to growth. List is ranked by year-to-date performance. 

No. 10: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) -7.3% YTD.

No. 9: American Express (NYSE:AXP) +1.1% YTD.

No. 8: Prologis (NYSE:PLD) +1.8% YTD.

No. 7: S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) +2.8% YTD.

No. 6: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG) +4.9% YTD.

No. 5: Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) +6.5% YTD.

No. 4: T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS) +6.6% YTD.

No. 3: Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) +6.8% YTD.

No. 2: Mastercard Corporation (NYSE:MA) +8.8% YTD.

No. 1: Progressive Corporation (NYSE:PGR) +16.1% YTD. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

For further exposure towards dividends and growth, investors can turn towards exchange traded funds for further diversification.

Dividend ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VIG), (NYSEARCA:SCHD), (NYSEARCA:VYM), (NYSEARCA:DGRO), (NYSEARCA:SDY), (NASDAQ:DVY), (NASDAQ:DGRW), (BATS:NOBL), (NYSEARCA:HDV), (NYSEARCA:FVD), (NASDAQ:RDVY), (NASDAQ:VIGI), and (NASDAQ:SDVY).

Growth ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VUG), (NYSEARCA:IWF), (NYSEARCA:IVW), (NYSEARCA:SCHG), (NYSEARCA:SPYG), (NASDAQ:VONG), (NYSEARCA:MGK), (NASDAQ:IUSG), and (NYSEARCA:VBK).

data.title: Sigma Lithium targets 20% cost reduction for all-in sustaining costs to $530 per ton by 2026 as expansion advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sigma Lithium (SGML) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Ana Cabral, Chief Executive Officer, opened by stating, "Over the last two years, we have transformed Sigma from an emerging producer to a leading global lithium company. Our core strategic advantage is our resilience to lithium price cycles." Cabral highlighted operational efficiency with a focus on lowering production costs, noting, "We delivered production volumes on target at 68,300 tons. This demonstrates the operational efficiency of the Greentech industrial plant."
  • Cabral emphasized the company's position as a low-cost producer, pointing out, "Our all-in sustaining costs, outperforming our targets by 6%. We are now at $622 a ton. This is the quantification of our resilience, and these costs are amongst the lowest in the industry."
  • The CEO stressed the company's ability to generate positive cash flow and repay expensive, short-term trade financing debt, adding, "We generated positive cash flow from operations which translates into this operational resilience."
  • Cabral also revealed that "100% of our production is uncommitted, which brings the potential to receive prepayments from signing offtake agreements with clients."
  • On safety, Cabral shared, "We have achieved over 700 days with zero accidents with lost time."
  • Irina Axenova, Vice President of Investor Relations, stated, "We reported $48 million in revenue, representing a 28% increase year-over-year driven by higher sales volumes and disciplined execution across the operation."
  • Axenova added, "EBITDA for the quarter was $10 million, and when adjusted for non-cash stock-based compensation, adjusted EBITDA reached $11 million... We reported our first net income of nearly $5 million or $0.04 per share."

Outlook

  • Cabral explained the expansion plan: "We have completed approximately 32% of the construction by this first quarter." The CEO further stated, "Building the second production plant is expected to be achieved with very low capex. It’s one of the lowest in the industry - it’s 0.40."
  • She signaled, "When we double production capacity, we become even more resilient to the lithium price cycles because we further lower our all-in sustaining costs by 20% from $660, which is the target guidance, down to $530, which is the target guidance for 2026."
  • The company expects to receive the first disbursement from its BNDES loan after submitting reimbursement documentation, which will drive equipment orders for the new plant.

Financial Results

  • Axenova reported, "Cost of sales came in at $34 million, reflecting a 19% increase year-over-year driven by higher production volumes, partially offset by lower operating costs. As a result, we delivered solid cash gross margin of 35%."
  • She stated, "We ended the quarter with a cash position of $31 million."
  • During the quarter, trade finance debt was reduced by 15%, and interest per ton dropped to $17. Total financial cost for the quarter was $75 per ton, with expectations to decrease to around $70 per ton for the full year.
  • Operational cash flow reached $24 million, with pro forma cash from operations at $17 million after expenses.

Q&A

  • Rock Hoffman, Bank of America, asked if the production of 5.0% spodumene concentrate was a conscious decision and about market preference for higher grades. Cabral replied it was deliberate: "The current pricing formula for lithium concentrate... decreases the base price linearly, but while we add the reprocessed material through the recycling, we gain mass exponentially, so it makes sense to actually... join both circuits in the Greentech plant to add mass exponentially, which significantly lowers our costs."
  • Hoffman followed up on further cost optimization. Cabral stated, "We prefer not to guide the gains with scale by furthering our operating costs... because we are already amongst the lowest in the industry."
  • Shannon Gill, Cormark Securities, asked about the timeline for the first BNDES loan draw and contingency plans. Cabral answered, "The first tranche, the reimbursements have been submitted, so the ball is in their camp... The repayment of short term debt makes sense with cash generation, and the decision is made on a quarterly basis depending on how much extra cash we have and depending on lithium market prices."
  • Gill also asked about prepayment via offtake. Cabral said, "We haven’t signed any offtake agreements to date, so 100% of our annual production... is fully available."
  • Katie Lachapelle, Canaccord Genuity, inquired about the percentage and duration of production to be covered by offtakes or prepayments. Cabral responded, "We have flexibility. I was given flexibility by the Board to sign the offtakes... These offtakes mean that we get a prepayment not for all of the amount of the future sales but for a certain percentage, and then typically there’s a grace period to repay this."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on operational strategy and financing flexibility, with a neutral tone and constructive follow-ups on cost and debt management.
  • Management showed high confidence in operational achievements and future cost reductions. Cabral’s remarks included, "We are obsessed with cost cutting... it’s a culture of efficiency that we have implemented at Sigma."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence remained high, and analysts maintained a neutral, fact-seeking approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language continues to project cost reductions, with the new target of $530 all-in sustaining cost for 2026 and ongoing expansion of production capacity.
  • Management focus shifted slightly more toward financing flexibility, highlighting the uncommitted production and potential for offtake prepayments as an untapped funding source.
  • Analysts remained focused on cost structure, debt management, and timing of major financing events, with less concern about production shortfalls compared to last quarter.
  • Key financial metrics showed resilience despite a decrease in sales, with EBITDA and net income improving.
  • Management’s tone remained confident, emphasizing efficiency and resilience, while analysts continued with a neutral, inquisitive tone.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company acknowledged pending receipt of the BNDES loan disbursement, with the timing dependent on reimbursement processing.
  • Cabral discussed flexibility in managing short-term liquidity based on cash generation and lithium prices.
  • Analysts questioned timelines for financing and contingency plans in case of delays.
  • Management pointed to the flexibility of uncommitted production and the ability to secure offtake agreements with prepayments as risk mitigants.

Final Takeaway

Sigma Lithium emphasized its industry-leading operational efficiency and low-cost position, highlighting positive cash flow, a robust safety record, and strategic flexibility with uncommitted production volumes. The company underscored its plan to nearly double production capacity while targeting a 20% reduction in all-in sustaining costs to $530 per ton by 2026, driven by scale and disciplined cost management. Management sees strong demand, robust financial performance, and an ability to leverage prepayment offtake agreements as key strengths supporting future growth and resilience.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
